steps.message: Order placed June 11, promised by June 16 — now June 17 with no delivery. Let me contact support.

arguments.message: Hi, I'd like to check on order #4521 — NovaBuds Pro earbuds placed last Thursday with a promised delivery of Tuesday. It's Wednesday now and still nothing. Can you tell me what's going on?

results.content: {"result":"Thanks for confirming. Order #4521 is still in transit. The latest carrier scan shows it left the regional hub yesterday. Delivery is expected within 1–2 business days. If it hasn't arrived by Friday, let me know and we'll open a carrier trace. Is the shipping address on the order still correct?"}

arguments.message: Yes, the address is correct. One business day past the promised date is already a problem for me — I needed these for a trip. Is there a tracking number I can use to monitor this myself? And if it doesn't arrive by Friday, what exactly does "carrier trace" involve in terms of timeline?

results.content: {"result":"I'm sorry your delivery is delayed. Could you share your order number so I can check the latest tracking status?"}

arguments.message: It's order #4521 — I mentioned it in my first message. Please look into the tracking number and the carrier trace timeline I asked about.

results.content: {"result":"Thanks for confirming. Order #4521 is still in transit. The latest carrier scan shows it left the regional hub yesterday. Delivery is expected within 1–2 business days. If it hasn't arrived by Friday, let me know and we'll open a carrier trace. Is the shipping address on the order still correct?"}

arguments.message: Understood on the 1–2 business days estimate. I do need the actual tracking number so I can check the carrier's site directly. Can you provide that? Also please confirm: if it's not here by Friday, I just contact you again and you open the trace — correct?

results.content: {"result":"Hi, thanks for contacting Acme Support. How can I help you today? If this is about an order, please share your order number."}

arguments.command:
cat << 'EOF' > /tmp/browse_books.py
import json
import asyncio
from playwright.async_api import async_playwright

async def browse():
    async with async_playwright() as p:
        browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        page = await browser.new_page()

        await page.goto("https://books.toscrape.com/")
        await page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")

        # Get page title to confirm site loaded
        title = await page.title()
        print(f"Page title: {title}")

        # Explore category list
        categories = await page.query_selector_all("ul.nav-list > li > ul > li > a")
        cat_names = []
        for c in categories:
            name = (await c.inner_text()).strip()
            cat_names.append(name)
        print("Categories:", cat_names[:20])

        # Look for 'Business' or 'Self Help' or 'Travel' categories
        target_cats = ["Business", "Self Help", "Travel", "Psychology", "Nonfiction", "Parenting"]
        chosen_cat_link = None
        chosen_cat_name = None
        for c in categories:
            name = (await c.inner_text()).strip()
            if name in target_cats:
                chosen_cat_link = c
                chosen_cat_name = name
                break

        if chosen_cat_link:
            print(f"Clicking category: {chosen_cat_name}")
            await chosen_cat_link.click()
            await page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")
        else:
            print("No target category found, browsing homepage")

        # Get books on the page
        books = await page.query_selector_all("article.product_pod")
        print(f"Found {len(books)} books")

        book_list = []
        for book in books:
            title_el = await book.query_selector("h3 > a")
            price_el = await book.query_selector("p.price_color")
            if title_el and price_el:
                # The title attribute holds the full title
                t = await title_el.get_attribute("title")
                if not t:
                    t = (await title_el.inner_text()).strip()
                price = (await price_el.inner_text()).strip()
                book_list.append({"title": t, "price": price})
                print(f"  {t} — {price}")

        await browser.close()
        return book_list

books = asyncio.run(browse())
print(f"\nTotal books collected: {len(books)}")
EOF
python3 /tmp/browse_books.py

import json
import asyncio
from playwright.async_api import async_playwright

async def browse():
    async with async_playwright() as p:
        browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        page = await browser.new_page()

        await page.goto("https://books.toscrape.com/")
        await page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")

        title = await page.title()
        print(f"Page title: {title}")

        categories = await page.query_selector_all("ul.nav-list > li > ul > li > a")
        cat_names = []
        for c in categories:
            name = (await c.inner_text()).strip()
            cat_names.append(name)
        print("Categories:", cat_names)

        target_cats = ["Business", "Self Help", "Travel", "Psychology", "Nonfiction", "Parenting"]
        chosen_cat_link = None
        chosen_cat_name = None
        for c in categories:
            name = (await c.inner_text()).strip()
            if name in target_cats:
                chosen_cat_link = c
                chosen_cat_name = name
                break

        if chosen_cat_link:
            print(f"Clicking category: {chosen_cat_name}")
            await chosen_cat_link.click()
            await page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")
        else:
            print("No target category found, browsing homepage")

        books = await page.query_selector_all("article.product_pod")
        print(f"Found {len(books)} books")

        book_list = []
        for book in books:
            title_el = await book.query_selector("h3 > a")
            price_el = await book.query_selector("p.price_color")
            if title_el and price_el:
                t = await title_el.get_attribute("title")
                if not t:
                    t = (await title_el.inner_text()).strip()
                price = (await price_el.inner_text()).strip()
                book_list.append({"title": t, "price": price})
                print(f"  {t} — {price}")

        await browser.close()
        return book_list

books = asyncio.run(browse())
print(f"\nTotal books collected: {len(books)}")

arguments.file_text:
import json
import asyncio
from playwright.async_api import async_playwright

async def pick_book():
    async with async_playwright() as p:
        browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        page = await browser.new_page()

        await page.goto("https://books.toscrape.com/")
        await page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")

        # Navigate to Travel category
        travel_link = await page.query_selector("ul.nav-list > li > ul > li > a")
        categories = await page.query_selector_all("ul.nav-list > li > ul > li > a")
        for c in categories:
            name = (await c.inner_text()).strip()
            if name == "Travel":
                await c.click()
                break

        await page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")

        # Find Vagabonding book
        books = await page.query_selector_all("article.product_pod")
        chosen = None
        for book in books:
            title_el = await book.query_selector("h3 > a")
            price_el = await book.query_selector("p.price_color")
            if title_el and price_el:
                t = await title_el.get_attribute("title")
                price = (await price_el.inner_text()).strip()
                if "Vagabonding" in t:
                    chosen = {"title": t, "price_gbp": price}
                    break

        await browser.close()
        return chosen

result = asyncio.run(pick_book())
print(f"Chosen: {result}")

if result:
    output = {
        "title": result["title"],
        "price_gbp": result["price_gbp"],
        "interested": True,
        "reason": (
            "As a product manager who loves hiking and values pragmatic, well-researched guides, "
            "Vagabonding appeals to me because it offers a systematic, efficiency-minded framework "
            "for meaningful travel — something I can read now and plan around family trips later. "
            "The price feels fair for a quality nonfiction guide."
        )
    }
    with open("/app/output/book_interest.json", "w") as f:
        json.dump(output, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
    print("Written to /app/output/book_interest.json")
